card.intuition: A system that aggregates and displays updates from friends or followed accounts in chronological order, involving complex fan-out strategies.
card.analogy: A newspaper subscription: in a 'Push' model (Fan-out on Write), the journalist delivers the paper to every subscriber's door. In a 'Pull' model (Fan-out on Read), the subscriber walks to the newsstand to check for updates themselves.
card.time_complexity: O(1) to read (with fan-out on write)
when_to_use: Building Facebook, X (Twitter), or LinkedIn feeds
Recommendation feeds based on user follows
gotchas: The 'Celebrity' problem: if a user with 50 million followers posts, pushing that update to 50 million caches (Fan-out on Write) can crash the system. Hybrid models are needed.
Consistency vs Availability: feeds usually prioritize availability. It's okay if you see a post 1 second later than your friend.

exercise.prompt: Which strategy is more efficient for users with MILLIONS of followers: Fan-out on Write (Push) or Fan-out on Read (Pull)?
hints: You don't want to 'Push' to 50M people at once
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "read"
assert check("Read")
print("PASS")
